changelog: FIX (DVR black-screen, ACTUAL root cause via PlaybackTelemetry) — Telemetry surfaced ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException(length=1, index=1) inside Media3 1.4.1's Mp4Extractor on every recording. Root cause: ffmpeg's +faststart+frag_keyframe+empty_moov MP4 muxer + MPEG-TS source produces files with thousands of stsc entries and edit lists that overflow Mp4Extractor's internal arrays. Fix: switched DVR recording format to MPEG-TS (.ts) — the canonical streaming-capture container. ExoPlayer's TsExtractor handles TS files trivially (15+ years of production reliability). Server now writes /home/dvr/recordings/{user}/{rec}.ts files, stream endpoint returns Content-Type: video/mp2t. Verified end-to-end: 26 s test recording produced a clean .ts file, ffprobe parses h264+aac perfectly, stream endpoint serves correct Content-Type, first byte is the TS sync byte 0x47.

Removed the v1.44.32 client-side MimeTypes.VIDEO_MP4 hint — let ExoPlayer pick the extractor automatically from the server's Content-Type. This way TS captures route to TsExtractor, legacy .mp4 files route to Mp4Extractor, and any future format works without a client release.
PlaybackTelemetry from v1.44.34 stays — proved its worth this round, will be invaluable for any future regressions.
Mandatory:true so older clients pull the new APK on next launch.
